title: Flawed Merge Sort on Doubly Linked List

statement.markdown: Given a doubly linked list with head node 'head', implement a function to merge sort it in-place, returning the sorted list's head node. Note that it is not necessary to store the whole sorted list in memory.
statement.inputFormat: The function will be given the head of a doubly linked list. The list will contain between 1 and 10000 integers, with each integer ranging from -5000 to 5000.
statement.outputFormat: Return the head of the sorted linked list.
statement.notes: It is acceptable to modify the original list.

examples.input: Example input (matching the stdin format that main wrapper expects)

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: void mergeSort(node* &head, node* &tail); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public void mergeSort(node head, node tail); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def mergeSort(self, head: Node, tail: Node) -> None:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: void Solution::mergeSort(node* &head, node* &tail) { if (!head) return; node* temp = head; node* prev; node* curr = head; while (curr) { prev = curr; curr = curr->next; if (curr) prev->next = curr->next; } mergeSort(temp, prev); }
buggyStarterCode.java: public void Solution.mergeSort(node head, node tail) { if (head == null) return; node temp = head; node prev; node curr = head; while (curr != null) { prev = curr; curr = curr.next; if (curr != null) prev.next = curr.next; } mergeSort(temp, prev); }
buggyStarterCode.python: def Solution.mergeSort(self, head: Node, tail: Node) -> None: if not head: return; temp = head; prev = None; curr = head; while curr: prev = curr; curr = curr.next; if curr: prev.next = curr.next; self.mergeSort(temp, prev);
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function Solution.mergeSort(head, tail) { if (!head) return; let temp = head; let prev; let curr = head; while (curr) { prev = curr; curr = curr.next; if (curr) prev.next = curr.next; } self.mergeSort(temp, prev); }

functionSignature.java: public void mergeSort(node head, node tail);
functionSignature.python: def mergeSort(self, head: Node, tail: Node) -> None:

testCases.input: First line count, second line elements (stdin match)
